scope: Enrich MiroFish domain graph nodes with inferred fit and retention semantics, then rerun the enterprise cluster playoff and diagnostic

decision_context.prior_state: The enterprise cluster diagnostic showed different bottlenecks across questionnaire, renewal, compliance evidence, and RFP, but the graph layer was still emitting domain nodes without explicit behavioral tags or retention priors.
decision_context.decision: Infer domain_tags and retention_score directly from opportunity text and candidate context, rerun the symmetric enterprise cluster playoff, and save a tuned diagnostic brief plus methodology note.
decision_context.why_now: Methodology tuning should happen before any more benchmark-promotion decisions, and the simulation should not keep evaluating enterprise domains with flattened graph semantics.

files_expected.purpose: Adds inferred domain fit tags and retention priors to graph domain nodes.
Stores the rerun of the symmetric enterprise cluster playoff after graph enrichment.
Stores the tuned diagnostic brief for the enterprise cluster.
Summarizes the graph-tuning rationale and the before/after enterprise ranking shift.
Captures the request, verification plan, and guardrails for this tuning tranche.

planned_steps: Enrich graph domain nodes with inferred behavioral tags
Enrich graph domain nodes with inferred retention priors
Rerun the symmetric enterprise cluster playoff
Build a tuned diagnostic brief from the rerun
Save the methodology note and update the transparent logs

expected_checks: The tuned run changes enterprise cluster ordering relative to the earlier symmetric playoff
The tuned diagnostic exposes whether each enterprise domain is now primarily a conversion or persistence problem
The methodology note records that benchmark admission remains deferred

guardrails: Do not auto-admit any enterprise domain into maintained benchmarks
Keep the tuning inside the MiroFish graph layer and research artifacts
Treat this tranche as methodology correction rather than promotion
